Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Law Office Of Robert B. Jobe, P.C.
Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KUDRAT CHAUDHARY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Law Office Of Robert B. Jobe, P.C.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to plaintiff's request for production (set one) as to defendant Law Office of Robert Jobe, P.C. is denied as moot. The record reflects that since plaintiff filed this motion, supplemental responses have been provided. (Whang Decl., Exs. 1 and 2; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pac. Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App. 4th 390, 408-09 [finding that a trial court has discretion in deciding whether to rule on a motion to compel after a party serves a response].)

The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the propriety of the supplemental responses. If necessary, plaintiff may file a new motion to compel further response.

Plaintiff is awarded $5,000 from defendant because there was no substantial justification for his initial inadequate response and delay. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a) ["The court may award sanctions under the Discovery Act in favor of a party who files a motion to compel discovery, even though ... the requested discovery was provided to the moving party after the motion was filed"].)

Proposed order signed and filed.

Judge: Victor M. Hwang; Clerk: K.Valdes; Not reported. (302/VMH)
